prevalence.source: ECDC/WHO Europe scabies context; dermatology surveillance
prevalence.trend: stable; outbreaks in long-term care and shelters

incidence.source: Outbreak investigations and primary care data

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; itch burden and secondary infection risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis based on pruritus and distribution; dermoscopy/microscopy for confirmation when needed

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands across the EU (outbreak-dependent)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with coordinated outbreak response and contact treatment

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Dermatology and primary care pathways with confirmatory testing available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical permethrin or oral ivermectin; treat close contacts; environmental cleaning protocols in institutions
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Coordinating outbreak control in institutional settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong public health outbreak response capacity and access to effective therapies
